task.title: Temporarily setting log levels at run time using nmcli

context.p: You can change the log level at run time using nmcli.

step.cmd: Optional: Display the current logging settings:

info.codeblock: # nmcli general logging LEVEL DOMAINS INFO PLATFORM,RFKILL,ETHER,WIFI,BT,MB,DHCP4,DHCP6,PPP,WIFI_SCAN,IP4,IP6,A UTOIP4,DNS,VPN,SHARING,SUPPLICANT,AGENTS,SETTINGS,SUSPEND,CORE,DEVICE,OLPC, WIMAX,INFINIBAND,FIREWALL,ADSL,BOND,VLAN,BRIDGE,DBUS_PROPS,TEAM,CONCHECK,DC B,DISPATCH

step.cmd: To modify the logging level and domains, use the following options:

li.p: To set the log level for all domains to the same LEVEL, enter:
li.codeblock: # nmcli general logging level LEVEL domains ALL

li.p: To change the level for specific domains, enter:
li.codeblock: # nmcli general logging level LEVEL domains DOMAINS
li.p: Note that updating the logging level using this command disables logging for all the other domains.
To change the level of specific domains and preserve the level of all other domains, enter:
li.codeblock: # nmcli general logging level KEEP domains DOMAIN:LEVEL,DOMAIN:LEVEL
